quiz_items.question: A power switch alone can satisfy the beam attenuator requirement for Class IIIb/IV laser products.
quiz_items.explanation: False. Power switches and key controls do not satisfy the beam attenuator requirement. The attenuator must reduce beam to Class I levels without turning off the laser and be available at all times during operation.

quiz_items.question: What is required for viewing optics (viewports, microscopes) on laser products?
options: Must provide access to Class IIIa levels for better visibility
May not provide access above Class I limits during operation/maintenance
Must be made of glass only
quiz_items.explanation: Viewing optics may not provide human access to laser or collateral radiation exceeding Class I and Table VI limits during operation or maintenance. Shutters/attenuators must be fail-safe.
